content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN); node 111 is the root. For each valid iii, the iii-th node has a value viv_ivi​ and another parameter mim_imi​.

A leaf is a node without sons. Let's denote the number of leaf nodes in the tree by LLL and their numbers by l1,l2,…,lLl_1, l_2, \ldots, l_Ll1​,l2​,…,lL​, in increasing order. Then, for each valid iii, let's define the answer for the leaf lil_ili​ in the following way:


Consider the path from the root to lil_ili​. For each node on this path (including the root and this leaf), choose a non-negative integer and multiply the value of this node by it.
Sum up the resulting values of all nodes on this path.
The answer aia_iai​ for this leaf is defined as the maximum possible remainder of this sum modulo mlim_{l_i}mli​​.
Find the answers for all leaves.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that nodes xxx and yyy are connected by an edge.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers v1,v2,…,vNv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Nv1​,v2​,…,vN​.
The last line contains NNN space-separated integers m1,m2,…,mNm_1, m_2, \ldots, m_Nm1​,m2​,…,mN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing LLL space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aLa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_La1​,a2​,…,aL​.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are only two leaves, l1=4l_1 = 4l1​=4 and l2=5l_2 = 5l2​=5. The answers for them are a1=0a_1 = 0a1​=0 and a2=9a_2 = 9a2​=9.
